signatures.label: print(*objects, sep=' ', end='\n', file=sys.stdout, flush=False) -> None

documentation.value: Prints objects to the text stream file, separated by sep and followed by end.
The objects to print. Non-string objects are converted using str().
String inserted between values. Default is a space.
String appended after the last value. Default is newline.
A file-like object (stream). Defaults to sys.stdout.
Whether to forcibly flush the stream.
Returns the number of items in an object. The argument may be a sequence (string, bytes, tuple, list, range) or a collection (dict, set, frozenset).
A sequence or collection object.

signatures.label: range(stop) -> range / range(start, stop, step) -> range

documentation.value: Returns an immutable sequence of numbers. Commonly used for looping a specific number of times in for loops.
The start value (inclusive). Default is 0.
The stop value (exclusive).
The step increment. Default is 1. Cannot be zero.

signatures.label: type(object) -> type / type(name, bases, dict) -> type

documentation.value: With one argument, returns the type of an object. With three arguments, creates a new type (class).
The object whose type is returned.
Returns True if the object is an instance of classinfo (a class, type, or tuple of classes/types).
The object to check.
A class, type, or tuple of classes and types to check against.

signatures.label: open(file, mode='r', buffering=-1, encoding=None, errors=None, newline=None, closefd=True, opener=None) -> IO

documentation.value: Opens a file and returns a corresponding file object. It is good practice to use with statement for proper cleanup.
A path-like object giving the pathname to open.
The mode: 'r' (read), 'w' (write), 'x' (create), 'a' (append), 'b' (binary), 't' (text), '+' (read/write).
The text encoding to use (e.g., 'utf-8'). Only used in text mode.
How encoding errors are handled: 'strict', 'ignore', 'replace', 'backslashreplace'.
Performs string formatting using replacement fields delimited by braces {}.
Positional arguments referenced by {0}, {1}, or {} in order.
Keyword arguments referenced by {name} in the format string.
Appends an object to the end of the list.
The object to append.
Returns the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.
The key to look up.
The value to return if the key is not found. Defaults to None.
Updates the dictionary with key/value pairs from other or from keyword arguments, overwriting existing keys.
A dictionary or iterable of key/value pairs.
Additional key=value pairs to update with.
Returns a new sorted list from the items in iterable.
The iterable to sort.
A function of one argument that extracts a comparison key (e.g., key=str.lower).
If True, sort in descending order.
Returns an enumerate object yielding pairs of (index, value) from the iterable.
The iterable to enumerate.
The starting index value. Default is 0.
Returns an iterator of tuples, where the i-th tuple contains the i-th element from each of the argument iterables.
Two or more iterables to zip together.
If True, all iterables must have the same length (Python 3.10+).
Returns an iterator that applies function to every item of iterable, yielding the results.
The function to apply to each element.
The iterable whose elements are passed to function.
Optional additional iterables. Function must accept that many arguments.
Returns an iterator yielding those items of iterable for which function returns True.
A function that returns True/False, or None to remove falsy values.
The iterable to filter.
Joins one or more path components intelligently, inserting OS-appropriate separators.
The base path.
Additional path components to join. An absolute component discards all previous components.

signatures.label: json.dumps(obj, *, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=True, indent=None, separators=None, default=None, sort_keys=False) -> str

documentation.value: Serializes a Python object to a JSON formatted string.
The Python object to serialize (dict, list, str, int, float, bool, None).
Number of spaces for pretty-printing indentation. None for compact output.
If True, dictionary keys are sorted alphabetically.
A function called for objects that are not serializable. Should return a serializable object.

signatures.label: json.loads(s, *, cls=None, object_hook=None, parse_float=None, parse_int=None) -> Any

documentation.value: Deserializes a JSON string to a Python object.
The JSON string to deserialize.
Optional function called with the result of every JSON object decoded. Used to customize decoding.
